title: duranta repens f. integrifolia
intro: Duranta repens f. integrifolia, also known as Golden Dewdrop, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Duranta genus in the Verbenaceae family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is often used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. The herbal preparations of Duranta repens f. integrifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
